title: br sync --flush-only silently no-ops when dirty flag not set by CLI subprocess
description: When br close/update is called via execFileSync subprocess (as foreman does in task-backend-ops.ts), the SQLite dirty flag is apparently not set. As a result, 'br sync --flush-only' reports 'Nothing to export (no dirty issues)' even though the JSONL is stale. Only '--force' actually exports. Root cause is likely that the dirty flag is tracked per-connection or not written by the CLI close command path. Fix options: (1) foreman always uses '--force' in its sync calls, or (2) br fixes dirty flag persistence when closing via CLI. Short-term workaround: use 'br sync --flush-only --force' in foreman.

title: Troubleshooter skill: detect and recover hung Pi SDK sessions
description:
Add a skill to the troubleshooting agent (bd-yo2r) that detects agents hung waiting on Pi SDK API responses and recovers them.

## Problem
Agents frequently hang in finalize or developer phases — the Pi SDK session waits for an API response that never comes (likely rate limit or network timeout). The process stays alive at 0% CPU with no progress updates. Currently requires manual detection (checking lastActivity timestamp) and manual kill.

## Detection
- Compare lastActivity timestamp in run progress to current time
- If stale > configurable threshold (e.g. 10 minutes with no tool calls), the session is hung
- Process is alive (pid exists) but at 0% CPU

## Recovery
- Kill the hung process (SIGTERM, then SIGKILL if needed)
- Mark run as stuck with a descriptive note (hung_api_session)
- If the run was in finalize and earlier phases succeeded, attempt to re-run just finalize
- If in an earlier phase, reset to open for full re-dispatch

## Integration
- This is a skill for the troubleshooter agent (bd-yo2r)
- Could also be integrated into foreman monitor / sentinel for proactive detection
- The sentinel already runs on a timer — adding a lastActivity staleness check would catch these early

title: foreman reset: MQ entry inserted before Pi runs, causing immediate merge-failed on re-dispatch
description:
## Problem

When `foreman reset --seed <id>` is run and the bead is immediately re-dispatched, the refinery inserts a merge queue entry the moment the worktree is created (before Pi runs). If any previous MQ entries from prior failed runs were not fully cleared, or if the new run's empty branch is picked up by the refinery poll loop before Pi has a chance to commit, the merge fails immediately with `reason: no-commits`.

Observed pattern in bd-aw5t:
- `worktree-created` at T+0s
- `bead-claimed` at T+1s  
- `merge-failed` (no-commits) at T+1s — Pi never ran

## Root Cause

The dispatcher marks the run `completed` and inserts a MQ entry immediately when the pipeline finishes. If the previous run left a `conflict` or `completed` MQ entry that `foreman reset` didn't remove (race between reset and a new dispatch), the refinery picks it up in the next poll cycle and tries to merge an empty branch.

Additionally, `foreman reset` removes MQ entries for the old run IDs but a new MQ entry is created for the fresh run before Pi has committed anything.

## Expected

`foreman reset` should ensure the merge queue is fully clear for the seed before the run is considered ready to dispatch. The MQ entry should only be inserted after the pipeline phases complete, not at worktree creation.

## Workaround

Manually clear the MQ with:
```bash
sqlite3 .foreman/foreman.db "DELETE FROM merge_queue WHERE seed_id='<id>';"
```
Then reset the bead status and re-dispatch.
notes: Branch foreman/bd-yd8x has no unique commits beyond dev. The agent may not have committed its work. Manual intervention required — do not auto-reset.

title: Implement troubleshooting agent for non-merged pipeline outcomes
description:
Build a specialized Pi SDK agent that activates when a pipeline run ends in any status other than 'merged'. Instead of blind reset-and-retry, the troubleshooter diagnoses the failure mode and applies targeted fixes.

## Failure Mode Skills

1. **fix-tests** — test-failed: read QA_REPORT.md, identify failing tests, apply fixes, re-run QA
2. **resolve-conflict** — conflict/rebase_conflict: parse conflict markers, resolve, retry finalize
3. **retry-push** — push_failed: clean stale remote branches, retry push
4. **close-completed** — nothing_to_commit/review with no commits ahead: verify work is on target branch, close bead
5. **retarget-pr** — pr-created with wrong target: close PR, retarget, re-push
6. **reset-stuck** — stuck with no clear cause: gather diagnostics, escalate or reset

## Architecture

- New workflow YAML phase: `troubleshooter` — triggered on failure, not part of normal sequence
- Dispatched from `onPipelineComplete` callback when status != merged/completed
- Has read access to all artifacts from the failed run (DEVELOPER_REPORT.md, QA_REPORT.md, REVIEW.md, mail, logs)
- Pi SDK tools registered: `fix-tests`, `resolve-conflict`, `retry-push`, `close-bead`, `foreman-status`
- Low max-turns budget (15-20) to avoid spiraling — escalate to human after 2-3 attempts
- Read-only on other worktrees, full access to its own

## Guardrails

- Cannot force-push or delete branches outside its worktree
- Cannot modify other beads or runs
- Must log all actions to TROUBLESHOOT_REPORT.md
- Escalates to human (via mail + bead note) if it cannot resolve in budget
- Tracks retry count per failure mode to avoid infinite loops

## Integration Points

title: finalize() calls closeSeed() unconditionally even when git push fails
description: In agent-worker.ts finalize(), closeSeed() at line 623 is called unconditionally. If git push fails (pushSucceeded=false, line 578-589), the branch is not on origin, no merge will ever happen, but the bead is still closed. The branch is also not enqueued to the merge queue. Run status is set to 'completed' which is wrong — the branch never left the worktree. Fix: guard closeSeed() behind if (pushSucceeded). In the push-failed branch, call resetSeedToOpen() to leave the bead in a retryable state.

title: Single-agent worker mode does not reset bead to open on failure
description:
Pipeline mode calls markStuck() which calls resetSeedToOpen() so failed beads reappear in br ready for retry. Single-agent mode (non-pipeline) does not call markStuck() — it updates the SQLite run to failed/stuck directly without touching the bead. The bead remains in its current status (likely 'in_progress') rather than being updated.

The correct behavior mirrors pipeline mode:
- Transient error (rate limit) → reset to 'open' + comment
- Permanent failure → set to 'failed' + comment with error summary

Fix: single-agent failure paths in agent-worker.ts should call the same failure handling as markStuck() including bead status update and comment.

title: Finalize should rebase onto target + re-run tests before pushing — catch merge-induced failures
description:
The QA⇄Developer loop runs tests in the isolated worktree, but the refinery runs tests on the merge result (worktree + dev). Tests can pass in isolation but fail after merge because another bead changed dev.

This causes beads like bd-m130 to pass QA, push, then fail in the refinery — with no way to loop back to the developer.

Fix: Add a pre-push validation step in finalize (or as a new 'validate' phase):
1. git fetch origin && git rebase origin/dev (already done)
2. npm test (NEW — run tests after rebase, before push)
3. If tests fail after rebase: send feedback to developer, loop back to dev→QA
4. If tests pass: push and proceed to merge

This catches merge-induced test failures while the pipeline is still active and can retry. The workflow YAML could control this:

```yaml
- name: finalize
  prompt: finalize.md
  prePushValidation:
    command: npm test
    retryWith: developer
    retryOnFail: 1
```

Alternative: add a 'validate' phase between reviewer and finalize that rebases and runs the full test suite.

title: autoMerge retry loop: sentinel beads cycle indefinitely when merge tests fail on pre-existing failures
description: When a sentinel bead fixes tests on its feature branch but autoMerge runs the test suite against the merge result (which includes other pre-existing failures on dev), the merge fails. The run gets marked failed, bead reset to open, dispatcher re-dispatches, and the cycle repeats. This burned multiple pipeline runs on bd-tg9l and bd-qgrr. Fix options: (1) autoMerge should only run tests affected by the branch's changes, not the full suite, (2) add a max retry count per bead that prevents infinite re-dispatch, (3) sentinel beads should be exempt from post-merge test validation.

title: shouldn't the workflow send mail between every step?  bead moved to in-progress->create worktree->explore->developer->qa->review->merge->merged should all send mail and the next step should depend on the previous step succeeding
description:
Add Agent Mail messages for the lifecycle events that currently have no mail coverage:

## Currently missing mail coverage
- bead moved to in_progress (dispatcher claims bead) → send mail to foreman
- worktree created → send mail to foreman  
- merge completed successfully → send 'merge-complete' from refinery to foreman
- merge failed (test failure, conflict, no commits) → send 'merge-failed' from refinery to foreman
- bead closed after successful merge → send mail to foreman

## Currently covered (do not duplicate)
- explorer phase complete/error → phase-complete / agent-error to foreman
- developer phase complete/error → phase-complete / agent-error to foreman  
- QA phase complete/error → phase-complete / agent-error to foreman
- reviewer phase complete/error → phase-complete / agent-error to foreman
- finalize enqueue failed → agent-error to foreman (just added)
- branch ready for merge → branch-ready to refinery

## Key files
- src/orchestrator/dispatcher.ts — dispatch + worktree creation
- src/orchestrator/agent-worker.ts — finalize phase (bead close happens in refinery, not here)
- src/orchestrator/refinery.ts — merge completion and failure paths
- src/lib/sqlite-mail-client.ts — mail client API

## Acceptance criteria
All lifecycle state transitions send an observable mail message so 'foreman inbox' provides a complete audit trail of what happened to a bead.

title: finalize: non-fast-forward push failure causes infinite sentinel retry loop
description:
When git push fails with 'non-fast-forward' during finalize, the run is marked stuck and resetSeedToOpen() resets the bead to open. The sentinel/dispatcher immediately re-dispatches, all phases are skipped (artifacts exist), push fails again, repeat. bd-qtqs accumulated 151 stuck runs in ~20 minutes from this loop.

Root cause: finalize() returns false on any push failure → runPipeline() calls resetSeedToOpen() unconditionally → bead goes open → dispatcher re-dispatches with no backoff.

Non-fast-forward is a deterministic failure (diverged history), not a transient error. Retrying without resolving the divergence will always fail.

Observed error:
  ! [rejected] foreman/bd-qtqs -> foreman/bd-qtqs (non-fast-forward)
  error: failed to push some refs

Local had 5 commits, origin had 1, branches had diverged.

Fix options:
1. Attempt git pull --rebase before push in finalize()
2. Classify non-fast-forward as 'conflict' status (not stuck) to skip resetSeedToOpen
3. Add exponential backoff in dispatcher before re-dispatching a seed that was recently stuck
notes: [FAILED] [REVIEWER] Claude Code executable not found at /Users/ldangelo/Development/Fortium/foreman/node_modules/@anthropic-ai/claude-agent-sdk/cli.js. Is options.pathToClaudeCodeExecutable set?

title: Unify prompt loader: install prompts to .foreman/prompts/ via init, enforce via doctor
description:
## Problem

There are currently two separate prompt loading systems:
- `loadAndInterpolate()` reads from `src/orchestrator/templates/` (hardcoded path, bundled in dist)
- `prompt-loader.ts` reads from `~/.foreman/prompts/{workflow}/` then `src/defaults/prompts/{workflow}/`

Smoke prompts live in `src/defaults/prompts/smoke/`, real prompts in `src/orchestrator/templates/`. This is confusing and error-prone.

## Design

### Single loader
Unify to one loader that resolves prompts from:
1. `<projectRoot>/.foreman/prompts/{workflow}/{phase}.md` (project-local override)
2. `~/.foreman/prompts/{phase}.md` (user global override)
3. Error — no fallback to bundled defaults at runtime

### foreman init
Copy all bundled prompt templates (explorer, developer, qa, reviewer, sentinel, smoke/*) to `<projectRoot>/.foreman/prompts/` on first init. Skip if already present unless `--force` is passed.

### foreman doctor
Add a `checkPrompts` check that:
- Verifies all required prompt files exist in `.foreman/prompts/`
- Errors if any are missing
- With `--fix`: reinstalls missing prompts from bundled defaults

### Error behavior
If a required prompt file is missing at runtime (pipeline phase start), throw a clear error:
  `Missing prompt for phase 'developer'. Run 'foreman init' or 'foreman doctor --fix' to reinstall.`

## Files
- `src/lib/prompt-loader.ts` — unified loader
- `src/orchestrator/template-loader.ts` — to be replaced/removed
- `src/orchestrator/roles.ts` — update to use unified loader
- `src/cli/commands/init.ts` — add prompt installation step
- `src/orchestrator/doctor.ts` — add checkPrompts
- `src/orchestrator/templates/` → move to `src/defaults/prompts/default/`
- `src/defaults/prompts/smoke/` → stays as `src/defaults/prompts/smoke/`

## Acceptance criteria
- `foreman init` installs all prompts to `.foreman/prompts/`
- `foreman doctor` lists missing prompts as errors
- `foreman doctor --fix` reinstalls missing prompts
- Pipeline throws a clear error if a prompt file is missing
- Smoke and default prompts use the same loader codepath
